header_summary:
Recent discussions in the Bitcoin development community have highlighted a proposal for EC public key recovery within the P2MR structure (BIP-360), aiming to reduce witness size by 35 bytes by eliminating the script-containing witness element, enhancing quantum resistance in Bitcoin's cryptographic framework. This optimization suggests a strategic shift towards more efficient and secure cryptographic practices, potentially reducing transaction costs and simplifying witness structures ([read more](https://delvingbitcoin.org/t/public-key-recovery-for-ec-leaves-in-p2mr-bip-360/2603)).

The slow adoption of new Bitcoin output types like P2MR and P2TRv2, which offer potential quantum resistance, has been analyzed, revealing that despite incentives, technologies like P2TR still cover only a minor fraction of the UTXO set. This discussion reflects concerns about the high costs and barriers associated with transitioning to quantum-safe outputs, which may hinder widespread adoption unless these outputs become cost-neutral or cheaper ([detailed insights](https://research.mempool.space/utxo-set-report/)).

In another vein, blockchain dynamics on testnet4 indicate significant discrepancies between reported and actual hashing difficulties, showing that a large percentage of blocks are confirmed through network propagation rather than mining competition. This operational insight underscores the challenges and the need for protocol adjustments to maintain network integrity and functionality under non-ideal conditions ([more information](https://gnusha.org/pi/bitcoindev/aiKeSdrUDP8N1Xww@erisian.com.au/T/#u#maa0776cab8affd1ccef44b688735dad9990a56d9)).

recent_posts.title: Aligning privacy incentives in P2MR
recent_posts.summary:
- The BIP-360 proposal suggests a 35-byte witness reduction by omitting script elements.
- Slow adoption of quantum-resistant formats like P2MR and P2TRv2 is linked to costs and previous tech uptake rates.
- Debates focus on ensuring Bitcoin survives a quantum event with minimal operational disruption.

recent_posts.title: Weak Quantum Bounty Ceremony
recent_posts.summary:
- A method utilizes quantum capabilities to validate without Zero-Knowledge protocols.
- Collision risk on 160-bit curves is reduced by fixing a variable in proofs.
- Quantum adversaries might delay theft until they can compromise keys covertly.

recent_posts.title: Testnet 4 difficulty/hashrate
recent_posts.summary:
- Testnet4 shows discrepancies between reported and actual blockchain hashing difficulties.
- About 90% of confirmations derive from network propagation, not mining competition.
- A ratio of 1 real block per 10-12 min-difficulty blocks maintains network integrity.

recent_posts.title: Public key recovery for EC leaves in P2MR (BIP-360)
recent_posts.summary:
- The proposal enhances BIP-360 by introducing a recoverable EC leaf in P2MR structures.
- This optimization shrinks witness size and avoids worsening quantum exposure risks.
- Modifications include changes to witness validation and a secure nonce derivation process.

recent_posts.title: Quantum Attack Game Theory
recent_posts.summary:
- The analysis explores blockchain decision-making and complex probabilistic theories.
- Intricate mathematics facilitate understanding strategies in cryptocurrency mining.
- A coupling argument shows probabilistic scenarios in blockchain are isomorphic.

recent_posts.title: P2WOTS: Live Signet & Interactive Wallet Demo
recent_posts.summary:
- The P2WOTS initiative enhances Bitcoin's quantum resistance with extensive testing and community participation.
- A user-friendly demo and wallet interface on a custom signet are available for public testing.
- The project’s GitHub hosts the latest codebase, encouraging community contribution and feedback.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] P2P UTXO Set Sharing
active_posts.summary:
- This BIP introduces new mechanisms for sharing UTXO sets within the Bitcoin network.
- It aims to streamline node setup and enhance network resilience by decentralizing UTXO distribution.
- The proposal includes new P2P messages and a Merkle tree-based integrity verification method.

active_posts.title: [BIP Draft] Testnet 5
active_posts.summary:
- A new Testnet5 draft aims to improve usability and test BIP54 simultaneously.
- Feedback on the draft is requested via inline comments or the Bitcoin Development Mailing List.
- The proposal is part of broader efforts to enhance Bitcoin's functionality and user experience.

active_posts.title: Prohibit Merkle Internal Node Preimages That Encode Minimal 64-Byte Transactions
active_posts.summary:
- The proposal introduces a rule to invalidate specific 64-byte Bitcoin transactions.
- It aims to prevent wrong SPV malleability by distinguishing transaction structures.
- Implementation guidelines advise miners on handling block composition issues.

active_posts.title: BIP-110 update: v0.4.1 release and implementation submitted to Bitcoin Core
active_posts.summary:
- BIP-110 v0.4.1 restricts data field sizes in Bitcoin transactions to enhance functionality.
- The updated client has been stable and introduces new consensus rules for scriptPubKeys.
- BIP-110 compatibility and implementation are supported across various platforms and nodes.

active_posts.title: Bird of Prey 2: non-malleable Schnorr + PQ signatures
active_posts.summary: - The paper presents 'BoP-2', a hybrid post-quantum signature scheme.  - 'BoP-2' enhances security by combining Schnorr and an arbitrary PQC scheme.  - This method improves unforgeability and non-malleability but increases signature size.

active_posts.title: Default signet reorg history — was the fork at height 287766 (depth 19) deliberate?
active_posts.summary:
- The investigation highlights key insights on reorgs in Bitcoin's signet testnet.
- Developers lack historical data on signet reorgs, impacting transparency and traceability.
- It's crucial to clarify if signet reorgs are expected behavior or rare anomalies.

today_in_history_posts.title: OP_BEFOREBLOCKVERIFY - discussing and opcode that invalidates a spend path after a certain block
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- BT proposed OP_BEFOREBLOCKVERIFY to allow switch-off transactions at a specific block.
- The new opcode may spur issues like mempool spam and reorganization problems.
- BT seeks community feedback on the OP_BBV bip detailed in his article.

today_in_history_posts.title: Waiting SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT and Packing Packages
today_in_history_posts.summary:
- The post identifies two Lightning network security issues: pre-signed feerate and transaction pinning.
- Proposed solutions include implementing SIGHASH_ANYPREVOUT and enhancing mempool resistance.
- Early deployment of package-relay in Bitcoin Core versions 0.24 or 0.25 is recommended.

today_in_history_posts.title: Deflationary money is a Good Thing
today_in_history_posts.summary: - Passive investment is acquiring assets without active involvement, like storing gold or land.  - Active investment engages directly, such as developing land or managing rental properties.  - The blog proposes central banks could stabilize economies using a mix of currencies, including Bitcoin.
